summary: The concept of an "early adopter" is indirectly supported by observed trends such as rapid increases in prescriptions of newer drugs like opioids and novel formulations replacing outdated treatments. Such behavioral shifts suggest some patient groups or clinicians quickly accept new therapies prior to widespread use, indicating potential existence within real-world pharmacological practice despite no explicit documentation.
